description:
src/lib.rs:306-321 checks CRC32C only 'if let Some(crc_str) = json.get("payload").and_then(...)'. If GCP omits payload.dataCrc32c (or returns it as a non-string type), the check is silently skipped and the secret is returned without integrity verification.

GCP Secret Manager docs (https://cloud.google.com/secret-manager/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.secrets.versions/access) state SecretPayload.dataCrc32c is always populated in successful responses. If GCP changes this in the future, or if a man-in-the-middle returns a response missing the field, the integrity check disappears with no warning to the caller or test failure.

Choices:
  (a) Require dataCrc32c on all successful responses; missing -> SecretError::DecodeFailed('GCP SM response missing required dataCrc32c'). Strictest, catches API drift immediately.
  (b) Keep optional but log/warn at debug level when absent. Mid-strict.
  (c) Document the silent-skip behavior in the integrity-check call site (currently has no comment explaining why optional).

Option (a) preferred — the documented invariant should be enforced or weakened deliberately. The integrity check is a defense; a defense that silently disappears is worse than no defense at all (false sense of security).

Trigger: any successful response with payload.dataCrc32c absent or non-string.
Actual: CRC check skipped, SecretValue returned unchecked.
Expected: documented and tested behavior either way.
title: CRC32C integrity check silently optional; future API drift could disable it
